title: STANDARD FOR COCOA POWDERS (COCOAS) AND DRY MIXTURES OF COCOA AND SUGARS CXS 105 - 1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2010, 2013, 2016, 2022.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR COCOA POWDERS (COCOAS) AND DRY MIXTURES OF COCOA AND SUGARS CXS 105 - 1981 Adopted in 1981. Revised in 2001. Amended in 2010, 2013, 2016, 2022.
sections.summary: Gives the standard title and revision history for cocoa powders and dry mixtures (CXS 105-1981) and records a 2022 amendment replacing prior non-retail container labelling text with a requirement that labelling of non-retail containers be in accordance with CXS 346-2021.
Declares the scope: the standard applies to cocoa powders (cocoas) and dry mixtures of cocoa and sugars intended for direct consumption.
Describes cocoa products and introduces definitions for cocoa powders derived from cocoa cake.
Defines 'Cocoa Powder', 'Fat-reduced Cocoa Powder' and 'Highly Fat-reduced Cocoa Powder' as products obtained by transforming cocoa cake into powder (see the Standard for Cocoa Mass and Cocoa Cake).

Sets essential composition and quality factors including a moisture limit (no more than 7% m/m), classification and minimum cocoa/cocoa-butter contents for cocoa powders and dry mixtures, defines chocolate powder (minimum 32% m/m cocoa powder), and lists optional ingredients (e.g., spices).
States the essential composition requirements, reiterating moisture and product-classification criteria for cocoa powders and dry mixtures, and the chocolate powder definition.
Specifies a maximum moisture content of 7% m/m for the products covered by the standard.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Cocoa Powder and Dry Mixtures of Cocoa and Sugars
sections.summary: Classifies cocoa powders by cocoa-butter content (Cocoa Powder ≥20% m/m; Fat‑reduced ≥10% and <20% m/m; Highly Fat‑reduced <10% m/m) and sets minimum cocoa-powder content categories for various dry mixtures and sweetened mixes based on percentage ranges (e.g., not <25% m/m, not <20% m/m, and <20% m/m).
Defines 'Chocolate Powder' as a mixture of cocoa powder and sugars and/or sweeteners with a minimum cocoa powder content of 32% m/m (29% m/m on a dry matter basis).
Lists permitted optional ingredients for the products as spices and salt (sodium chloride).

Permits acidity regulators, anticaking agents, bulking agents, emulsifiers, stabilizers, sweeteners and thickeners in accordance with Tables 1–3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) for cocoa mixes and cocoa mass/cake, and requires flavourings to comply with CXG 66-2008 with only flavourings that do not imitate chocolate or milk allowed at GMP; only specified Table 3 additives are acceptable.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015) and other relevant Codex texts, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Sets naming and labelling rules: product names must follow the standard (e.g., 'Chocolate Powder'), minimum cocoa powder content must be declared where required, sweetener use affects product naming (e.g., must use 'sweetened' or 'artificially sweetened' rather than 'sugared'), and products with less than 25% total cocoa powder shall not be designated 'chocolate'; labelling of non-retail containers must follow CXS 346-2021.

Specifies methods of analysis/sampling: methods for determination of cocoa butter and of full‑fat/fat‑reduced cocoa powders are to be developed, while moisture determination is specified as AOAC 977.04 or IOCCC 26 (1988) Karl Fisher method.
